senses.definition: past tense of refocillate, meaning to bring back or restore focus or attention to something, often after a distraction
examples: The lecturer refocillated the audience after a long aside.
She refocillated her efforts on the main task.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refocillate; to bring back or restore focus or attention to something
examples: The speaker refocillates the audience with a vivid example.
The program refocillates the team on the project goals.

senses.definition: present participle of refocillate; bringing back or regaining focus or attention
examples: She is refocillating the class with a compelling question.
The discussion is refocillating toward the main issue.

senses.definition: the act or result of refocillating; the process of restoring focus or attention
examples: The refocillation of the audience occurred after the speaker paused.
A brief refocillation helped regain the class's concentration.

senses.definition: plural form referring to multiple instances of refocillation
examples: The coach noted several refocillations during the drill.
There were brief refocillations to reestablish engagement.

senses.definition: to change the focus or attention to a different thing
examples: She refocuses the camera on the subject.
We need to refocus our efforts on the core goals.

senses.definition: the act of changing focus or a new focus
examples: The film's refocus came after the midpoint twist.
The refocus allowed for clearer storytelling.

senses.definition: past tense of refocus; brought back or changed focus to a different thing
examples: He refocused his attention on the assignment.
The team refocused after the initial setback.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refocus; changes focus to something else
examples: She refocuses the discussion on the main issue.
The device refocuses the image automatically.

senses.definition: present participle of refocus; the act of changing focus to something else
examples: The team is refocusing on quality control.
Refocusing occurred after new data emerged.

senses.definition: British spelling of refocused; changed focus to something else in the past
examples: They refocussed their strategy after the review.
The project was refocussed to meet client needs.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refocus in British spelling; changes focus to something else
examples: The committee refocusses its efforts on safety.
The app refocusses the image automatically.

senses.definition: British spelling of refocusing; the act of changing focus to something else
examples: The refocussing of the campaign helped reach a broader audience.
Refocussing occurred after new data was released.

senses.definition: to fold again or differently; to re-fold something
examples: Please refold the blanket neatly.
She refolds the map to fit it in the pocket.

senses.definition: past tense of refold; folded again or differently
examples: She refolded the napkins after the spill.
The garment was refolded for storage.

senses.definition: present participle of refold; the act of folding again
examples: The craftsman is refolding the fabric for symmetry.
Refolding the map helps fit it back into the wallet.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refold; folds again
examples: He refolds the shirt before packing.
The assistant refolds the diagram to fit the folder.

senses.definition: rare or obsolete term for reformation or renewal of mind or spirit
examples: The refoment of the era brought new ideas.
A refoment in opinion occurred among the scholars.

senses.definition: a new font or typeface, short for reformatted font; can be seen in design contexts
examples: The designer chose a refont for the poster.
They updated the document with a modern refont.

senses.definition: to fool again; to deceive anew
examples: They attempted to refool the audience with the same trick.
Be careful not to be refool by old scams.

senses.definition: to restore footing or position, literally or figuratively; to place feet back on the ground
examples: The climber tried to refoot after the slip.
The team needed to refoot their plan after the setback.

senses.definition: past tense of refoot; established footing again
examples: They refooted the ladder after a wobble.
The team refooted their strategy after the break.

senses.definition: the act of reestablishing footing or position
examples: The refooting of the team improved stability.
A new refooting process was adopted.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refoot; places feet again or establishes footing again
examples: He refoots the ladder before climbing.
The character refoots themselves after the stumble.

senses.definition: to prohibit or forbid again; to place a new or renewed prohibition on something
examples: The council will reforbid the use of certain chemicals.
They reforbid the practice after new evidence emerged.

senses.definition: to strengthen or support; to provide additional strength or support
examples: The team hired a medic to reforce the crew.
They reforcé their defense with new players.
